questions.prompt: Explain the principle that a word denotes its own form unless it has a technical-name function. Give one example and one limitation of the principle.
questions.student_notes: Answer from memory. Do not cite books or external sources unless already known.

questions.prompt: Define guna and vrddhi as technical vowel grades in Paninian grammar. Give the vowel sets included under each term and explain why these definitions matter in derivation.
What is a pada according to the standard Paninian definition? Explain how the definition covers both nominal and verbal forms.

In Paninian derivation, how is conflict between two applicable rules usually resolved by the later-rule principle? Explain the principle and one reason it cannot be applied mechanically without context.
Analyze the sandhi in 'devendraḥ'. Identify the likely underlying members and explain the phonological operation.
